title: Seeded Asteroid Generation, Polygons, Sizes, and Difficulty Inputs · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for seeded asteroid generation, polygons, sizes, and difficulty inputs and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for seeded readable asteroid factory. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.
Generate ordered angular vertices with bounded radius variation and validate minimum size, winding, and intersections.
Define large, medium, and small radius, speed, score, fragment, rendering, and collision contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Seeded Rng · changed case · Quiz Seeded Rng
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 3495 during an offline cross-platform release rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Inject a dedicated random generator and record seed and draw order without claiming cryptographic randomness.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Difficulty Inputs, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Difficulty Inputs precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Difficulty Inputs, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 9257 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Difficulty Inputs, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: Difficulty is one multiplier applied to every number.

stimulus.title: Difficulty Inputs failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Difficulty is one multiplier applied to every number.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.

options.text: Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Difficulty Inputs
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Difficulty Inputs: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 5828 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to generate readable seeded asteroid polygons under geometry, size, spawn, and difficulty budgets; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Difficulty Inputs.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Difficulty Inputs, keep Difficulty Inputs connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Bound count, speed, size, spawn interval, aim burden, cue clarity, and selectable assistance as separate difficulty inputs.
